address: Wat Pha Tak Suea, Pha Tang subdistrict, Sangkhom District, Nong Khai, Thailand
opening_hours: Official guidebook material describes daytime access; recheck exact local opening conditions before setting out.

description.en: The strongest headline viewpoint on the Sangkhom side, with a glass skywalk and wide Mekong views.
description.nl: Het sterkste headline-uitzichtpunt aan de Sangkhom-kant, met een glazen skywalk en breed uitzicht over de Mekong.

metaTitle.en: Wat Pha Tak Suea Skywalk | Go2Thailand
metaTitle.nl: Wat Pha Tak Suea Skywalk | Go2Thailand

metaDescription.en: Why Wat Pha Tak Suea Skywalk is the clearest Sangkhom-side viewpoint add-on from Nong Khai and how to plan it as a focused outing.
metaDescription.nl: Waarom Wat Pha Tak Suea Skywalk de duidelijkste viewpoint-uitstap vanuit Nong Khai is en hoe je die als gerichte outing plant.

enhanced_description:
Wat Pha Tak Suea Skywalk is the cleanest headline viewpoint on the Sangkhom side of Nong Khai province. The appeal is straightforward: you come for a glass cliff-edge platform, a broad Mekong panorama, and, in the cooler part of the year, the possibility of mist over the river landscape.

What makes the stop work editorially is restraint. This is best treated as the main reason for a focused Sangkhom outing, not as one item inside a bloated province loop that leaves no time for the city itself.
detailed_history: The provincial Nong Khai e-book and the Weaving Mekong guidebook both continue to frame Wat Pha Tak Suea through its temple setting, cliff-edge skywalk, and broad Mekong views over both Thailand and Laos.
visitor_experience: Expect a viewpoint-oriented stop rather than a long temple complex visit. The draw is the look-out point itself, so timing and weather matter more than packing the day with extra low-value stops afterward.

best_time_to_visit.crowd_level: Usually more comfortable outside late-morning heat

practical_info.how_to_get_there: Reached by road from Nong Khai city as part of a deliberate Sangkhom-side outing.
practical_info.nearby_attractions: Phu Huai Isan, Wat Hin Mak Peng, and other Sangkhom river-road stops, though not all need to be combined in one trip.
practical_info.food_options: Plan food before or after the main viewpoint block rather than assuming a strong meal scene on site.
practical_info.shopping: This is a viewpoint stop, not a shopping stop.

insider_tips: Choose this when you want the classic Nong Khai province viewpoint rather than multiple weaker add-ons.
Check local weather and road conditions before leaving, especially outside the cool season.
If the skywalk is the priority, do not overload the same outing with too many secondary stops.
verified_note: Source-backed summary based on the provincial Nong Khai e-book and the Weaving Mekong guidebook. Current local access conditions should still be checked before departure.
cultural_significance: The site matters because it connects Nong Khai's Sangkhom-side temple landscape with the wider Mekong river geography in one very legible stop.
